Activity 07: XGBoost Quantile Forecast

  • 1 Run The Workflow
  • 2 Load Outputs
  • 3 Model Design
  • 4 No-Look-Ahead Framing
  • 5 FluSight Forecast Output
  • 6 XGBoost Performance
  • 7 Comparison With Other Versions
  • 8 Interpretation
  • 9 Reproducibility Summary

Activity 07 tests XGBoost quantile-regression alternatives to the original ARIMA forecast. It keeps the same testing season, reference dates, horizons, target, location, and FluSight quantile ladder, but replaces auto.arima() with direct horizon-specific gradient-boosted trees.

Two XGBoost candidates are compared:

  • Admissions-only XGBoost: lagged influenza admissions plus target-week seasonal harmonics.
  • XGBoost + leading indicators: the same features plus NWSS WVAL and NSSP influenza ED visit signals lagged six weeks from the target week.

The leading indicators are lagged so that every feature is available before the forecast reference date.

knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE, warning = TRUE, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 6)

find_project_dir <- function(start_dir = getwd()) {
  candidates <- unique(c(
    start_dir,
    file.path(start_dir, "USE-ME", "carolmoreira"),
    file.path(start_dir, ".."),
    file.path(start_dir, "..", ".."),
    file.path(start_dir, "..", "..", "..")
  ))
  candidates <- normalizePath(candidates, mustWork = FALSE)
  candidates <- candidates[file.exists(file.path(candidates, "output/scripts/07_xgboost_forecast.R"))]
  if (!length(candidates)) stop("Could not locate USE-ME/carolmoreira.")
  candidates[1]
}

project_dir <- find_project_dir()
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = project_dir)
if (!isTRUE(getOption("knitr.in.progress"))) setwd(project_dir)
cat("Working directory:", project_dir, "\n")
## Working directory: /Users/carolmoreira/sismid/SISMID-2026-HumanAI/USE-ME/carolmoreira

1 Run The Workflow

The script below rebuilds the forecast, scores it, compares it with available benchmarks, and writes the figures used in this report.

source("output/scripts/07_xgboost_forecast.R")
## Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.4.3
## Warning: package 'xgboost' was built under R version 4.4.3

2 Load Outputs

required <- c("readr", "dplyr", "tidyr", "ggplot2", "knitr")
missing <- required[!vapply(required, requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE)]
if (length(missing)) stop("Required R package(s) not installed: ", paste(missing, collapse = ", "))

library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
forecast_csv <- "output/data/07_xgboost/xgboost_flusight_forecasts.csv"
leading_forecast_csv <- "output/data/07_xgboost/xgboost_leading_flusight_forecasts.csv"
scores_csv <- "output/data/07_xgboost/xgboost_forecast_scores.csv"
leading_scores_csv <- "output/data/07_xgboost/xgboost_leading_forecast_scores.csv"
summary_csv <- "output/data/07_xgboost/xgboost_summary_by_horizon.csv"
leading_summary_csv <- "output/data/07_xgboost/xgboost_leading_summary_by_horizon.csv"
comparison_csv <- "output/data/07_xgboost/xgboost_model_comparison_by_horizon.csv"
spec_csv <- "output/data/07_xgboost/xgboost_specification.csv"
diagnostics_csv <- "output/data/07_xgboost/xgboost_diagnostics.csv"
leading_diagnostics_csv <- "output/data/07_xgboost/xgboost_leading_diagnostics.csv"

forecast_png <- "output/figures/07_xgboost/xgboost_forecast_vs_observed.png"
leading_forecast_png <- "output/figures/07_xgboost/xgboost_leading_forecast_vs_observed.png"
comparison_png <- "output/figures/07_xgboost/xgboost_metric_comparison.png"
width_png <- "output/figures/07_xgboost/xgboost_interval_widths.png"
leading_width_png <- "output/figures/07_xgboost/xgboost_leading_interval_widths.png"
feature_png <- "output/figures/07_xgboost/xgboost_feature_overview.png"
leading_feature_png <- "output/figures/07_xgboost/xgboost_leading_feature_overview.png"

expected <- c(forecast_csv, scores_csv, summary_csv, comparison_csv,
              spec_csv, diagnostics_csv, leading_forecast_csv,
              leading_scores_csv, leading_summary_csv, leading_diagnostics_csv,
              forecast_png, leading_forecast_png, comparison_png,
              width_png, leading_width_png, feature_png, leading_feature_png)
if (!all(file.exists(expected))) {
  stop("Missing Activity 07 output(s): ", paste(expected[!file.exists(expected)], collapse = ", "))
}

3 Model Design

XGBoost has no built-in autoregressive time-series structure, so the weekly series is converted into a supervised learning table. For each reference date and horizon, both candidates use the last four known admissions values plus first and second seasonal harmonics for the target MMWR week. The leading candidate additionally uses WVAL and NSSP values from six weeks before the target week.

read_csv(spec_csv, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  kable(caption = "XGBoost quantile model specification.")
XGBoost quantile model specification.
model response external_regressors features framing objective quantile_levels eta max_depth subsample colsample_bytree nrounds seed
XGBoost quantile regression weekly national influenza admissions none lag1; lag2; lag3; lag4; sin1; cos1; sin2; cos2 direct per horizon; three models per reference date reg:quantileerror 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.3, 0.35, 0.4, 0.45, 0.5, 0.55, 0.6, 0.65, 0.7, 0.75, 0.8, 0.85, 0.9, 0.95, 0.975, 0.99 0.05 3 0.8 0.8 300 42
XGBoost quantile regression + leading indicators weekly national influenza admissions NWSS WVAL and NSSP influenza ED visits lagged six weeks from target week lag1; lag2; lag3; lag4; sin1; cos1; sin2; cos2; wval_lag6; nssp_flu_lag6 direct per horizon; three models per reference date reg:quantileerror 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.3, 0.35, 0.4, 0.45, 0.5, 0.55, 0.6, 0.65, 0.7, 0.75, 0.8, 0.85, 0.9, 0.95, 0.975, 0.99 0.05 3 0.8 0.8 300 42

4 No-Look-Ahead Framing

The XGBoost model is fit directly by horizon. For a reference date r and horizon h, training examples use only anchor weeks whose observed target t + 7h is no later than r. Prediction uses the anchor week r, so all lag features are known at forecast time.

diagnostics <- read_csv(diagnostics_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
leading_diagnostics <- read_csv(leading_diagnostics_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
bind_rows(diagnostics, leading_diagnostics) %>%
  group_by(horizon) %>%
  summarise(
    models = n_distinct(model),
    reference_dates = n_distinct(reference_date),
    min_training_rows = min(n_training_rows),
    max_training_rows = max(n_training_rows),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  kable(caption = "Training row counts by direct forecast horizon.")
Training row counts by direct forecast horizon.
horizon models reference_dates min_training_rows max_training_rows
1 2 34 58 298
2 2 34 58 297
3 2 34 58 296

5 FluSight Forecast Output

Each reference date emits 69 rows: 3 horizons times 23 quantiles. Quantiles are clamped at zero, rounded to integer counts, sorted to prevent crossing, and validated against the FluSight date and ladder rules.

forecasts <- read_csv(forecast_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
forecasts %>%
  arrange(reference_date, horizon, output_type_id) %>%
  head(12) %>%
  kable(caption = "Preview of the Activity 07 FluSight-format XGBoost forecast.")
Preview of the Activity 07 FluSight-format XGBoost forecast.
reference_date target horizon target_end_date location output_type output_type_id value
2025-09-27 wk inc flu hosp 1 2025-10-04 US quantile 0.010 355
2025-09-27 wk inc flu hosp 1 2025-10-04 US quantile 0.025 790

2025-09-27 wk inc flu hosp 1 2025-10-04 US quantile 0.200 1081
2025-09-27 wk inc flu hosp 1 2025-10-04 US quantile 0.250 1085
2025-09-27 wk inc flu hosp 1 2025-10-04 US quantile 0.300 1098
2025-09-27 wk inc flu hosp 1 2025-10-04 US quantile 0.350 1111
2025-09-27 wk inc flu hosp 1 2025-10-04 US quantile 0.400 1115
2025-09-27 wk inc flu hosp 1 2025-10-04 US quantile 0.450 1129
2025-09-27 wk inc flu hosp 1 2025-10-04 US quantile 0.500 1143 
leading_forecasts <- read_csv(leading_forecast_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
leading_forecasts %>%
  arrange(reference_date, horizon, output_type_id) %>%
  head(12) %>%
  kable(caption = "Preview of the Activity 07 XGBoost + leading indicators forecast.")
Preview of the Activity 07 XGBoost + leading indicators forecast.
reference_date target horizon target_end_date location output_type output_type_id value

6 XGBoost Performance

xgb_summary <- read_csv(summary_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
xgb_leading_summary <- read_csv(leading_summary_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
bind_rows(xgb_summary, xgb_leading_summary) %>%
  mutate(across(c(mae, rmse, mean_wis, coverage_95, mean_width_95), round, 3)) %>%
  arrange(horizon, mean_wis) %>%
  kable(caption = "XGBoost forecast performance by horizon.")
## Warning: There was 1 warning in `mutate()`.
## i In argument: `across(...)`.
## Caused by warning:
## ! The `...` argument of `across()` is deprecated as of dplyr 1.1.0.
## Supply arguments directly to `.fns` through an anonymous function instead.
## 
##   # Previously
##   across(a:b, mean, na.rm = TRUE)
## 
##   # Now
##   across(a:b, \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE))
XGBoost forecast performance by horizon.
model horizon n_forecasts mae rmse mean_wis coverage_95 mean_width_95
XGBoost quantile 1 34 2345.382 4662.489 1624.717 0.882 11729.79
XGBoost + leading indicators 1 34 2534.824 4918.606 1750.440 0.941 22074.29
XGBoost quantile 2 34 3348.647 6735.226 2280.391 0.853 14558.12
XGBoost + leading indicators 2 34 4884.176 8469.174 2924.441 0.941 27169.65
XGBoost quantile 3 34 4085.088 7532.189 2656.315 0.912 16049.15
XGBoost + leading indicators 3 34 6576.265 10712.137 3631.763 0.941 31572.79

7 Comparison With Other Versions

The comparison table includes both XGBoost candidates plus the original ARIMA baseline, Activity 5 ensemble, and Activity 8 final forecast when those outputs are available locally. In this run, adding leading indicators improved coverage but widened intervals enough to worsen WIS relative to admissions-only XGBoost.

comparison <- read_csv(comparison_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
comparison %>%
  mutate(across(c(mae, rmse, mean_wis, coverage_95, mean_width_95), round, 3)) %>%
  arrange(horizon, mean_wis) %>%
  kable(caption = "Activity 07 XGBoost compared with available forecast versions.")
Activity 07 XGBoost compared with available forecast versions.
model horizon n_forecasts mae rmse mean_wis coverage_95 mean_width_95
Activity 5 ensemble 1 34 1859.176 3446.426 1494.176 0.882 6243.500
Original ARIMA 1 34 1954.176 3404.723 1547.448 0.882 6257.294
XGBoost quantile 1 34 2345.382 4662.489 1624.717 0.882 11729.794
Activity 8 current final 1 34 2345.382 4662.489 1624.717 0.882 11729.794
XGBoost + leading indicators 1 34 2534.824 4918.606 1750.440 0.941 22074.294
XGBoost quantile 2 34 3348.647 6735.226 2280.391 0.853 14558.118
Activity 8 current final 2 34 3348.647 6735.226 2280.391 0.853 14558.118
XGBoost + leading indicators 2 34 4884.176 8469.174 2924.441 0.941 27169.647
Activity 5 ensemble 2 34 4364.676 7732.303 3484.610 0.853 12060.765
Original ARIMA 2 34 4407.706 7925.581 3531.953 0.853 12096.559
XGBoost quantile 3 34 4085.088 7532.189 2656.315 0.912 16049.147
Activity 8 current final 3 34 4085.088 7532.189 2656.315 0.912 16049.147
XGBoost + leading indicators 3 34 6576.265 10712.137 3631.763 0.941 31572.794
Activity 5 ensemble 3 34 6071.824 10939.742 4837.259 0.853 16627.265
Original ARIMA 3 34 6102.235 11355.899 4914.413 0.853 16682.618

8 Interpretation

XGBoost behaves differently from ARIMA-style models. It can learn nonlinear relationships between recent admissions, season timing, and future admissions, which helps at longer horizons here. Adding WVAL/NSSP did not improve WIS in this backtest, likely because the surveillance streams have a shorter aligned history and produced much wider intervals. This is still useful evidence: the leading signal is available, but it needs more careful regularization or a targeted ensemble before it should replace admissions-only XGBoost.

tibble::tribble(
  ~aspect, ~assessment,
  "Strength",
  "Strong two- and three-week WIS in this evaluation window.",
  "Strength",
  "Uses the exact same FluSight format and reference dates as the baseline.",
  "Strength",
  "Direct quantile regression allows asymmetric predictive distributions.",
  "Strength",
  "A leakage-safe WVAL/NSSP XGBoost variant is now tested directly.",
  "Weakness",
  "The leading-indicator variant widened intervals and worsened WIS in this run.",
  "Weakness",
  "Less interpretable than ARIMA or ARIMAX.",
  "Weakness",
  "Direct horizon models do not always produce intervals that widen monotonically."
) %>%
  kable(caption = "Summary of Activity 07 strengths and weaknesses.")
Summary of Activity 07 strengths and weaknesses.
aspect assessment
Strength Strong two- and three-week WIS in this evaluation window.
Strength Uses the exact same FluSight format and reference dates as the baseline.
Strength Direct quantile regression allows asymmetric predictive distributions.
Strength A leakage-safe WVAL/NSSP XGBoost variant is now tested directly.
Weakness The leading-indicator variant widened intervals and worsened WIS in this run.
Weakness Less interpretable than ARIMA or ARIMAX.
Weakness Direct horizon models do not always produce intervals that widen monotonically.

9 Reproducibility Summary

cat("Forecast CSV:", forecast_csv, "\n")
## Forecast CSV: output/data/07_xgboost/xgboost_flusight_forecasts.csv
cat("Leading forecast CSV:", leading_forecast_csv, "\n")

cat("Rows in forecast:", nrow(forecasts), "\n")
## Rows in forecast: 2346
cat("Rows in leading forecast:", nrow(leading_forecasts), "\n")
## Rows in leading forecast: 2346
cat("Reference dates:", dplyr::n_distinct(forecasts$reference_date), "\n")
## Reference dates: 34
cat("Rows per reference date:", nrow(forecasts) / dplyr::n_distinct(forecasts$reference_date), "\n")
## Rows per reference date: 69
